text: Setting clear goals is essential for any project to succeed. A useful approach is to use SMART goals, which are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," you might set a goal to "increase homepage visits by 20% within three months." Break your project into milestones, such as completing a design draft or finishing testing. Each milestone should have clear deliverables, like a report or a prototype. Finally, ensure stakeholder alignment by discussing these goals with everyone involved, such as team members or clients. When everyone agrees on the targets, collaboration becomes smoother and the project is more likely to stay on track.
